item.title: Introducing atmos-fx
item.description: A small zero-dependency atmosphere engine for creative weather-inspired UI effects.

item.title: What Should Software Engineers Do in the Age of Agents
item.description: A personal reflection on how AI agents lower the barrier to building software, and why software engineers still need stronger system and product thinking.

item.title: EnhanceGPT 0.1.0: A Small Extension for a Better ChatGPT Workflow
item.description: Announcing the first release of EnhanceGPT, a Chrome extension that adds conversation outline, bulk management, and prompt workflow improvements to ChatGPT.

item.title: What Rebuilding Classic Machine Learning Algorithms from Scratch Actually Taught Me
item.description: Rebuilding classic machine learning algorithms from scratch turned out to be less about formulas and more about numerical stability, data assumptions, and engineering tradeoffs.

item.title: Deploying OpenClaw on Windows WSL and Managing It from macOS
item.description: A practical setup for running OpenClaw inside WSL on a spare Windows machine and controlling it from macOS.

item.title: Recreating Top-Tier UI Philosophy with Claude Code
item.description: A workflow for translating visual taste into reusable engineering systems.

item.title: A Simple AGENTS.md Setup for Agent-Based Development
item.description: A small personal setup to reduce repetition by separating global defaults from project-specific rules.

item.title: Loading Large-Scale Point Data on Web Maps
item.description: An architecture comparison of server-rendered raster overlays and viewport-based vector loading for large-scale point data on web maps.

item.title: Building a Personalized Portrait Generator with Stable Diffusion, IP-Adapter, and LoRA
item.description: A concise note about building a local AI portrait generator that separates identity preservation, image generation, and style control.

item.title: New Beginning
item.description: Starting a new writing space for software and systems.

item.title: Building an E2E Testing Architecture for a Complex Micro-Frontend
item.description: A practical summary of how I structured E2E tests for a large micro-frontend project, including test goals, layered architecture, reusable cases, Page Objects, file validation, and lessons learned.

item.title: Building a Modern Micro-Frontend Architecture on Top of Legacy Systems
item.description: A practical architecture note on introducing a micro-frontend shell, runtime composition, shared context, and compatibility bridges while keeping legacy pages alive.

item.title: Seamlessly Moving an UNO Card Across Multiple Tabs
item.description: A practical demo showing how to move an UNO card seamlessly across multiple browser tabs.

item.title: Understanding the Execution Order of setTimeout, setImmediate, and process.nextTick in Node.js
item.description: A practical look at how timers, setImmediate, and nextTick behave in the Node.js event loop.
